pull.title: [diffusion] Fix RuntimeError in SageAttention3 on Nvidia Blackwell with Qwen-Image
pull.body:
## Motivation

On Blackwell (SM12x), `sglang generate` may auto-select SageAttention3 (`sage_attn_3`) when `sageattn3` is installed and no `--attention-backend` is provided. SageAttention3 assumes MHA (`num_heads == num_kv_heads`). For GQA/MQA models like `Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512` (28 query heads, 4 KV heads), this leads to a runtime error from a head-count mismatch.

This PR avoids SM12x/Blackwell crashes by (1) making SageAttention3 opt-in so it is not auto-selected by default, and (2) when `--attention-backend sage_attn_3` is explicitly requested, falling back to Torch SDPA (with `enable_gqa=True`) for GQA/MQA layers (`Hq != Hkv`) while continuing to use SageAttention3 for MHA layers (`Hq == Hkv`), addressing the first issue mentioned in https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/16302 (SageAttention3 failure), but not the second issue mentioned (NVML “Not Supported” failure)
## Modifications

- `python/sglang/multimodal_gen/runtime/layers/attention/backends/sage_attn3.py`: detect GQA/MQA (`Hq != Hkv`) and fall back to `torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(..., enable_gqa=True)`; keep SageAttention3 for MHA

- `python/sglang/multimodal_gen/runtime/platforms/cuda.py`: make SageAttention3 opt-in on SM12x by removing its auto-selection in the default backend path (still available when explicitly requested via `--attention-backend sage_attn_3`).

## Accuracy Tests

I verified that Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512 no longer crashes during the text encoding stage when running with `--attention-backend sage_attn_3`.

Tested on 1x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell WS

**Before the fix (main branch):**

```
sglang generate --model-path Qwen/Qwen-Image-2512 --prompt "Draw a picture of LA in the rain."
```

## Checklist

- [x] Format your code according to the [Format code with pre-commit](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#format-code-with-pre-commit).
- [ ] Add unit tests according to the [Run and add unit tests](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#run-and-add-unit-tests).
- [ ] Update documentation according to [Write documentations](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#write-documentations).
- [x] Provide accuracy and speed benchmark results according to [Test the accuracy](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#test-the-accuracy) and [Benchmark the speed](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#benchmark-the-speed).
- [x] Follow the SGLang code style [guidance](https://docs.sglang.io/developer_guide/contribution_guide.html#code-style-guidance).

repo.name: sglang
repo.description: SGLang is a fast serving framework for large language models and vision language models.

repo.name: sglang
repo.description: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework for large language models and multimodal models.

files.patch:
@@ -3,6 +3,7 @@
 # SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
 
 import torch
+import torch.nn.functional as F
 from sageattn3 import sageattn3_blackwell
 
 from sglang.multimodal_gen.runtime.layers.attention.backends.attention_backend import (
@@ -17,7 +18,6 @@
 
 
 class SageAttention3Backend(AttentionBackend):
-
     accept_output_buffer: bool = True
 
     @staticmethod
@@ -38,6 +38,7 @@ def get_metadata_cls() -> type["AttentionMetadata"]:
 
 
 class SageAttention3Impl(AttentionImpl):
+    _warned_gqa_fallback_global: bool = False
 
     def __init__(
         self,
@@ -63,6 +64,29 @@ def forward(
         query = query.transpose(1, 2)
         key = key.transpose(1, 2)
         value = value.transpose(1, 2)
-        output = sageattn3_blackwell(query, key, value, is_causal=self.causal)
+        # SageAttention3's Blackwell kernel assumes MHA (Hq == Hkv). For GQA/MQA
+        # (Hq != Hkv), fall back to torch SDPA which supports GQA.
+        if key.shape[1] != query.shape[1]:
+            if query.shape[1] % key.shape[1] != 0:
+                raise ValueError(
+                    "GQA/MQA requires query heads to be a multiple of KV heads, "
+                    f"got q_heads={query.shape[1]} and kv_heads={key.shape[1]}"
+                )
+            if not type(self)._warned_gqa_fallback_global:
+                logger.warning(
+                    "SageAttention3 does not support GQA/MQA (Hq != Hkv); falling back to torch SDPA."
+                )
+                type(self)._warned_gqa_fallback_global = True
+            output = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(
+                query,
+                key,
+                value,
+                is_causal=self.causal,
+                dropout_p=self.dropout,
+                scale=self.softmax_scale,
+                enable_gqa=True,
+            )
+        else:
+            output = sageattn3_blackwell(query, key, value, is_causal=self.causal)
         output = output.transpose(1, 2)
         return output

@@ -5,6 +5,7 @@
 """Code inside this file can safely assume cuda platform, e.g. importing
 pynvml. However, it should not initialize cuda context.
 """
+
 import os
 from collections.abc import Callable
 from functools import lru_cache, wraps
@@ -130,7 +131,6 @@ def get_available_gpu_memory(
         sm = capability.to_int() if capability else 0
 
         if sm in SHARED_SYSMEM_DEVICE_MEM_SMS:
-
             free_gpu_memory = psutil.virtual_memory().available
         else:
             free_gpu_memory, _ = torch.cuda.mem_get_info(device_id)
@@ -151,6 +151,7 @@ def get_attn_backend_cls_str(
         head_size: int,
         dtype: torch.dtype,
     ) -> str:
+        target_backend: AttentionBackendEnum | None = None
         # TODO(will): maybe come up with a more general interface for local attention
         # if distributed is False, we always try to use Flash attn
         if selected_backend == AttentionBackendEnum.SLIDING_TILE_ATTN:
@@ -187,6 +188,7 @@ def get_attn_backend_cls_str(
